task: M13 Exposure-Frequency Counter Engine + M14 Multi-Q Tagging + Propagation
notes: Pure-functional counter-based familiarity engine — replaces SRS. EXPOSURE_TARGETS = {short:6, medium:8, long:12}. RETIRE_WINDOW = 3 (last-N must be all 'correct'). State machine NEW -> IN-PROGRESS -> FAMILIAR; FAMILIAR is terminal within session (no demotion on subsequent wrongs). All public functions immutable: every mutator returns new SessionState; original never touched (verified by JSON-snapshot tests). Per-atom familiarity = round(correctExposures / sum-of-per-card-targets * 100), capped 0..100. Per-Q-track familiarity = unweighted mean of contributing atom percents (rounded). Multi-Q propagation is automatic: card is the unit of memory; qToAtoms index makes a single recordCardResult lift every tagged Q-track on the next query — no explicit fan-out. propagateExposure() adds drift assertion (supplied qTags must match registered, order-independent). rankCardsByCoverage() picks cards that lift the most below-target Q-tracks per drill (the concrete value of multi-Q tagging for the deck composer). Tests: 65 vitest cases across 9 categories; 100% line/statement/function coverage on both engines, 97% branch (uncovered = defensive ?? defaults). Stress test: 1000 random ops with deterministic LCG, all 8 invariants hold (I1 exposure>=correct, I2 seq=ops, I3 lastN<=RETIRE_WINDOW, I4 FAMILIAR terminal, I5 atom%∈[0,100], I6 Q% = mean(atoms%), I7 retire requires both target+last3, I8 status forward-only). Strict tsc clean (noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes). Zero runtime side effects, zero AI calls, zero localStorage — session-only state matches v2 SessionState contract from SCHEMA-1. Vitest + @vitest/coverage-v8 added to devDeps; npm scripts test/test:watch/test:coverage live alongside other agents' test:v2.

task: Q1 TraceCard v2 component + grading-trace lib + 5 stories
notes: VSCode-debugger 3-pane Q1 backbone. Layout: header (stem + atom-id + qTags + 4-stop confidence placeholder), left pane CodeEditor readOnly with active-line marker (▶ prefix on the highlighted line, computed via withActiveLineMarker over a copy of card.code so the source stays untouched for grading), right column splits 1.5fr/1fr into VariablesPanel (interactive — student adds rows manually, NO auto-execution) + TerminalPanel display + writable textarea below labelled 'predicted output'. Footer: [⏮ reset] [⏵ step N/total] [⏩ run] left-grouped + Submit primary + try-again ghost when failed. Step mode: activeLine starts null, click Step -> 1, increments, -1 = past end (button disabled). Run jumps to end (no highlight). Reset clears variables, terminal, activeLine, grade (full state reset). Submit calls gradeTrace; pass triggers onComplete(true), failure shows feedback panel with per-variable green/red rows + terminal diff (unified-ish +/-) + collapsible teach-me details. Submit disabled after pass to prevent accidental re-grade. Terminal pane is split: TerminalPanel (read-only frame for visual fidelity) + plain textarea so student types stdout free-form (one line per row); we split + normalizeLenient line-by-line at grade time. grading-trace.ts: pure deterministic grader. buildExpectedTrace walks expectedTrace steps and keeps LAST value per variable name (final state) — output-only steps (variable=='') skipped. gradeTrace returns {pass, varResults[], terminalCorrect, terminalDiff?, expectedTerminalText, actualTerminalText}. Variable comparison: case-sensitive name lookup, current-value = last history entry (fallback to .value), valuesEqualLenient via normalizeLenient. normalizeLenient: trim outer whitespace + collapse internal runs to single space + strip whitespace adjacent to 2-char ops (<<, >>, ==, !=, <=, >=, &&, ||) FIRST then 1-char (<, >, =, +, -, *, /, %) so '<<' isn't broken into two '<' + lowercase recognized keywords ONLY (true/false/nullptr/cout/endl/cin) — variable names + numeric literals stay case-sensitive per spec. Extra student variables ignored (allows tracking intermediates), missing required vars are wrong. terminalDiff: simple line-based +/- prefix. Stories: 5 fixtures hand-mirrored from cpp-t2/data/v2/cards/L0/F-03/trace-0[1-4].yml + 1 V2.0-style synthesised exam-realistic sum_positive: easy (3-line single int, mirrors trace-01), medium (for-loop sum 1..4 with i exit value 5 captured in expected trace), hard (struct holds array + dot-access mutation stats.total), edge (i <= 3 off-by-one trap, count=4 not 3), exam-realistic (sum_positive function called from main with array {3,-1,5,-2,4}, branch skips negatives, returns 12, prints 'sum: 12'). Each story tracks completion timestamp in local state. A11y per RULE 4: section role='application' + aria-label='Trace exercise — atom F-XX', step controls role='group' aria-label='step controls' with named per-button aria-labels, confidence rating role='group' with aria-pressed on each stop, terminal textarea aria-multiline+aria-label, FeedbackPanel role='status' aria-live='polite', code editor inherits role='application' from primitive with ariaLabel='C++ trace, active line N'. All buttons keyboard-reachable via Tab and activate on Space/Enter (button native behavior). teach-me details element collapsible with summary as keyboard target. Determinism: zero LLM calls, zero randomness in grading (Math.random only used for non-grading row-id keys), session-only state, normalizeLenient is pure; same {variables, terminalText} always yields same GradeResult. Decoupled from card-schema Zod via TraceCardLike subset interface so grading-trace.ts is unit-testable in isolation. Component-scoped CSS via inline <style> using --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-*/--font-mono with VSCode Dark+ fallbacks so it renders correctly even if loaded before theme.css.

task: Build TemplateRecallCard 3-stage component (STUDY -> HIDE -> TYPE -> GRADED) + skeleton-mask + line-match + 5 stories
notes: Verbatim recall drill for cpp-t2 Option 4. STAGE MACHINE: STUDY (3-8s flash, configurable studySeconds, auto-advance via 100ms interval OR press [I'm ready]) -> HIDE (skeleton hint generated by skeleton-mask, press [Recall]) -> TYPE (CodeEditor primitive, real-time per-line green-tick gutter via lineMatchVector, brace counter via braceBalance, line-by-line vs all-at-once mode toggle) -> GRADED (PASS/FAIL + side-by-side diff for fails + collapsible explanation). MODES: line-by-line (default first 3 encounters) shows active line marker (orange triangle); all-at-once (post 3 successes) just types and grades. SKELETON-MASK: pure function generateSkeleton(canonical, hideTypes? | options) reuses cpp-tokenizer; default hides identifier/number/string but ALWAYS keeps keywords (int/double/struct/...) + structural identifiers (main/cout/cin/endl/std); placeholder default '___'; collapses adjacent slot+ws+slot to single space-separated slots. Also exports generateIdentifierSkeleton for ident-only masking. LINE-MATCH: normalizeLenient strips ws around 19 C++ operators (longest-first <<= ... -> :: then single-char =/+/-/...) and collapses internal ws + trim; lineMatches/lineMatchVector for per-line ticks; gradeBlock returns {ok, matched, firstMismatch} and rejects extra non-blank lines beyond canonical; braceBalance ignores braces inside line-comments + string + char literals (escape-aware). A11Y: aria-live polite stage announcer, role=alertdialog skip-confirm modal (Esc gated by stage), tabIndex=-1 root for keyboard escape, autoFocus on submit-cancel default, sr-only counter readout. STYLE: inline component-scoped CSS using --bg-/--text-/--accent-/--state- theme tokens with safe fallbacks so the storybook renders before theme.css loads. STORIES: 5 plain-React stories matching CodeEditor.stories.tsx pattern (no Storybook dep yet) — struct definition, classic for-loop, pass-by-ref signature, nested brace-init, main() body. Each story carries a mode-toggle + last-result chip. Type-clean under project tsconfig flags (strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes + noUnused*). Smoke-test verified: skeleton(struct Point {double x;}) -> 'struct ___ { double ___; }' (keeps `double` keyword, masks identifier), match-vector + grade behave correctly on good/bad input, brace-balance respects strings/comments. Zero runtime AI calls, zero localStorage, session-only state — matches Option 4 contract.

task: Stage progression engine + 4 escape valves + failure-recovery (stage-gate engine)
notes: Pure-functional stage-progression engine for 6-stage Q-track ladder (S1 Tour 100% / S2 Template 95% / S3 Components 90% / S4 Compose 90% / S5 Variations 85% / S6 Speed 90%). task: Daily-deck composer + adaptive in-session deck + DAG backward retry (Option 4 §11.1)
notes: composeDailyDeck(allCards,state,dayNumber,targetCount,opts?) is the canonical pure-functional deck builder per spec §11.1. Algorithm: (1) phase = D1..D3 BLOCK / D4+ INTERLEAVE; dominantQ rotates Q1..Q4 by dayNumber. (2) Score each card with qWeight × stageWeight × statusWeight × stagePolicyMul × dominantBonus × ageBonus; lowest-accuracy Q-track gets 1.5× per spec. (3) Layered draw: 60% NEW(in-stage-band) + 30% IN-PROGRESS(<50%) + 10% FAMILIAR(sanity); top-up from overflow when underfilled. (4) Phase ordering: BLOCK groups by Q with dominant first and S1/S2 contiguous; INTERLEAVE greedy no-two-same-Q-back-to-back. (5) Catch-up tail = last 30 cards from atoms <50% across all 4 Qs. (6) Daily audit: every Q with stock must hold ≥ floor(target × 10%); under-represented Qs get up to 5 force-injected cards by evicting over-represented body cards. Determinism via custom LCG seeded with hash(seed,day,target,seq) — no Math.random anywhere. Stage-gate stub resolves currentStage as the highest exposed stage (real engine may swap in later). adaptiveAdjust(deck,state,recent,opts?): on miss → inject best-matching DeltaCard (atom-then-Q match); on hit → no-op; fatigue heuristic detects accuracy drop > 20% across 10-card windows and lightens the deck by deferring up to 3 heavy cards (AdversarialMockCard/TestDaySimCard/MainWriteCard/FunctionWriteCard/TraceCard) for lighter ones (MCQ/Demo/Cloze/Decompose/ConfidenceCalibration). dag-backward-retry: walkBackward() does BFS reverse-walk with cycle defense; injectPrereqCards() picks N cards across prereq atoms in BFS order with multi-pass cycling. Tests: 49 vitest cases across 3 spec files; 100% function coverage, 98.41% line coverage, 86.34% branch coverage on the three engines (uncovered lines are defensive fallbacks for empty pools / unregistered cards). Property-based fuzz across 50 seeds verifies length+uniqueness invariants for any random allCards. All 11 documented invariants D1..D11 hold. Strict tsc-clean (noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes). Pure functions, zero mutation of inputs, zero localStorage, zero AI calls — matches v2 SessionState contract.
